gaps.description: The Bill of Rights (BoR) exists only as a scaffold with placeholder articles (Articles I–VI marked "[To be defined by human principal]"). It contains no enterprise scope, no prohibited activities, no core identity, no human authority reservation, and no interpretation rules. The MSP explicitly states that Arbiter (#190) verifies the BoR at every scope query, and that the MSP translates the BoR into machine‑actionable objectives. With an empty BoR, Arbiter cannot enforce any scope gate, making the first compliance step inoperative. Nomos (#200) cannot map MSP rules to a constitutional identity because there is none. The entire governance chain is suspended on a missing foundational document.

The MSP 'Genome References' section lists no genome documents under genome/. The MSP rules section is also empty, but the genome is where organ‑specific operating rules and risk‑tier‑specific constraints are supposed to be expressed. Without any genome documents, organ behavior is unconstrained and default‑permissive beyond the empty BoR. This means that even after the BoR is defined, there are no per‑organ operational boundaries calibrated to the organism’s actual structure (e.g., no rules for Spine, Nomos, Radiant, etc.). The system is operating with a 'blank check' for all organ actions.

The MSP amendment protocol specifies that amendments originate from Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) issued by Nomos when an Action Proposal is blocked. So far there is no record of any PEMs, APs, or amendments in the World State (no concepts of type 'policy_exception_message', no msp_version beyond 1.0.0-seed). This means the entire amendment machinery — Senate‑Legislator review, HOM notifications, Rollout staging, canary, activation, rollback — has never been tested end‑to‑end. The governance mechanisms are unverified and may contain latent defects that would only surface when the first real policy conflict occurs.

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "OP-GAP-001",
      "description": "No organ availability SLA is defined. The 'Rules' section of the MSP is empty, providing no baseline for organ uptime, acceptable downtime, or recovery time objectives. The spine-state slice shows all organs frequently transitioning between ALIVE and DEGRADED with no governance on acceptable patterns.",
      "grounding_section": "MSP: Rules",
      "grounding_type": "MSP",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 9
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "OP-GAP-002",
      "description": "Missing pong response time target. No rule defines an expected response window for organs to reply to spine health checks (pings). The spine-state reveals rapid 'missed_pong' transitions, suggesting the current timeout may be too aggressive or undefined, leading to unnecessary DEGRADED states and operational noise.",
      "grounding_section": "MSP: Rules",
      "grounding_type": "MSP",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 8
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "OP-GAP-003",
      "description": "No cadence baseline for health-check intervals. The spine transition log shows high-frequency oscillations (multiple DEGRADED→ALIVE cycles per minute), indicating no defined baseline for how often spine should poll organs. This absence can cause state thrashing and mask genuine instability.",
      "grounding_section": "MSP: Rules",
      "grounding_type": "MSP",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 7
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "OP-GAP-004",
      "description": "Missing organ-specific runtime targets. The MSP 'Genome References' section is empty; no genome documents exist to differentiate critical organs (e.g., Nomos, Arbiter) from less critical ones. All organs are treated uniformly, despite differing operational significance, as evidenced by identical transition patterns across all organs.",
      "grounding_section": "MSP: Genome References",
      "grounding_type": "MSP",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "medium",
      "priority_score": 6
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "OP-GAP-005",
      "description": "BoR Article I (Scope of Enterprise) is undefined. Without a defined scope, the MSP cannot establish operational boundaries or priorities. This creates a risk that operations may inadvertently exceed intended limits, and there is no constitutional basis for any operational SLA tied to DIO purpose.",
      "grounding_section": "BoR: Article I — Scope of Enterprise",
      "grounding_type": "BoR",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "critical",
      "priority_score": 10
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "gap-001",
      "description": "BoR Article I (Scope of Enterprise) is empty. No relational boundary is defined: the persons, roles, and interactions covered by the DIO’s constitutional commitments are unspecified. Without this article, person-attention coverage has no anchor — the DIO cannot know whose needs and which relationship patterns must be protected by Nomos enforcement.",
      "grounds": "Bill of Rights — Article I (Scope of Enterprise) (scaffold, awaiting human authorship)",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-002",
      "description": "BoR Article II (Prohibited Activities) is empty. Leon’s observed relational expectations (e.g., requiring intent clarification before infrastructure changes, opposing unsolicited modifications) have no constitutional prohibition. Person-attention coverage lacks negative constraints; the DIO is not constitutionally prevented from ignoring Leon’s stated relational needs.",
      "grounds": "Bill of Rights — Article II (Prohibited Activities) (scaffold, awaiting human authorship)",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-003",
      "description": "BoR Article III (Core Identity) is empty. The DIO’s relational identity — how it presents itself as a collaborative agent, its commitments to transparency, consultation, and respect for human authorship — is undefined. The MSP cannot translate any identity-based rules for person-attention coverage.",
      "grounds": "Bill of Rights — Article III (Core Identity) (scaffold, awaiting human authorship)",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 4
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-004",
      "description": "BoR Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) is empty. There is no constitutional reservation of decisions that require human attention (e.g., infrastructure changes, IP protection decisions). Without this article, the DIO could autonomously bypass Leon’s authority, contradicting the person-attention patterns captured in Minder’s observations.",
      "grounds": "Bill of Rights — Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) (scaffold, awaiting human authorship)",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-005",
      "description": "MSP Rules section is empty, and while the Minder is rich with Leon’s relational patterns (e.g., requiring test results after every change, mapping governance branches to OTP processes, treating scripts as first-class citizens), no operational rule translates these into actionable person-attention coverage constraints. Nomos currently has no MSP gate to enforce Leon’s relational requirements, even on a default-permissive basis.",
      "grounds": "MSP — Rules section (currently empty) and the MSP’s role as the BoR-to-operational translator",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 5
    }
  ]
}
